terms.label: Memo
terms.definition: A versioned strategy document and the single highest authority over its own rollout; its content is local and MUST NOT be uploaded.
terms.negativeDelimitation: NOT a shareable or uploadable document, and NOT a cross-memo container — a memo has authority over its OWN rollout; the cross-memo span belongs to a goal or a plan.

terms.label: Revision
terms.definition: One immutable snapshot of a memo's content, stored as REV-XX.md; each change produces a new file, so the history is forward-only.
terms.negativeDelimitation: NOT an in-place edit — a revision is never mutated; a change is always a new REV-XX file.

terms.label: Topic
terms.definition: An atomic point extracted from the input during the five-step input pipeline; the complete topic list serves as a writing checklist and is the head of the Topic to Block to PRD chain.
terms.negativeDelimitation: NOT too small to omit and NOT cross-memo — extraction is exhaustive and topics are memo-scoped.

terms.label: Goal
terms.definition: A cross-memo intent (id G###) that spans several memos and outlives any one; defined by intent not surface, and scored in a fresh context against real state.
terms.negativeDelimitation: NOT scoped inside one memo and NOT surface-defined — a goal spans the memo sequence and is never scored in the session that did the work.

terms.label: Block
terms.definition: A structure node inside a memo carrying its own B### identifier; it references one or more topics and is the unit PRDs are derived from.
terms.negativeDelimitation: NOT a synonym for a context block — it is an addressable structure node with a B### id, not a chunk of prose context.

terms.label: PRD
terms.definition: A block-derived chunk of work sized so one PRD fits into one context (hard cap one third, smart-zone one quarter) and is implementable by one agent in one thread.
terms.negativeDelimitation: NOT a multi-thread or multi-context unit — if work needs a second thread, that is the signal to chunk into more PRDs, not to span one PRD across threads.

terms.label: Phase
terms.definition: A sequential, mandatory unit of the rollout that bundles PRDs executed together and in order, carrying an orchestration role and a mandatory depends-on.
terms.negativeDelimitation: NOT optional and NOT parallel by default — a phase is mandatory and sequential; depends-on is a required characteristic, not decoration.

terms.label: Strand
terms.definition: The emergent dependency closure over phases — the transitive closure of the depends-on edges, derived by walking the graph, never authored.
terms.negativeDelimitation: NOT authored and NOT a thematic bundle — a strand is emergent, and it is a tag, never part of the numeric memo ID.

terms.label: Requirement
terms.definition: A single addressable statement that must, should, or may hold; two-sided (statement faces generation, check faces the gate) with an optional grade axis, resolving to a ternary status.
terms.negativeDelimitation: NOT one-sided and NOT self-graded — a requirement faces both generation and the gate, a check that did not run MUST NOT report PASS, and the doer MUST NOT be the grader.

terms.label: Tool
terms.definition: An external capability a phase or PRD depends on, recorded as a descriptor in the per-project tools registry that says that a tool exists, what it is for, and where it lives.
terms.negativeDelimitation: NOT a runtime and NOT an enforced gate — the registry is descriptive and a RECOMMENDATION; a memo MUST NOT be blocked solely because no registry is present.

terms.label: Plan
terms.definition: The execution-side primitive above the single memo: it spans one or more finalized memos and sequences their phases into a single ordered run (id PLAN-###-slug).
terms.negativeDelimitation: NOT a replacement for the per-memo stage model — a plan nests the four stages per carried memo; and phase order stays the memo's to decide (memo authority).

terms.label: User Role
terms.definition: The interactive role in the harness tool-contract roles{} (10-harness-registry): the surface a developer drives directly; it adds the interaction tool to core.required[] and is the top-level trust reference the other two roles are bounded under.
terms.negativeDelimitation: NOT the same as a 'User session' type — the role is the tool-contract delta in roles.user, while the session type is the session-tier classification that anchors trust; and NOT a self-granted privilege.

terms.label: Orchestrator Role
terms.definition: The coordinating role in the harness tool-contract roles{} (10-harness-registry): it adds the coordination tools to core.required[] and carries the gate-dependent taskCrud marker; it runs work on the user's behalf and MUST NOT exceed the user's trust level.
terms.negativeDelimitation: NOT a new authority — the orchestrator is the user's context continued into coordination, bounded by monotonicity, never a separate higher privilege; and NOT the same as the agent-team 'Lead' name (13-orchestration), which is one deployment of this role.

terms.label: Worker Role
terms.definition: The scoped-execution role in the harness tool-contract roles{} (10-harness-registry): it adds structured-output and removes the coordination tools, executing one bounded unit of work in an isolated context.
terms.negativeDelimitation: NOT an orchestrator — a worker cannot coordinate a team or spawn one; and NOT a persistent identity — a worker is a scoped, one-unit execution, bounded below the orchestrator that started it.
